steps.title: Focus on the narrative opening
steps.content: The question asks for a choice that begins a narrative about Mara Oberon's life. Since the notes include details from her birth through late-career roles, the best opening should start at the beginning of the life story rather than summarize the whole career or jump to a later event.

steps.title: Set aside later career details
steps.content: Choice B, "Mara Oberon appeared in many films, including The Three Passions (1928) and Interval (1973), and was the first Indian-born actress to be nominated for an Academy Award," works more like a career summary. Choice C, "In 1973, Mara Oberon played the role of Serena Moore in the film Interval," begins near the end of her life and career, not at the start.

steps.title: Choose the biographical start
steps.content: Choice D, "Though she would go on to receive credit in many films, Mara Oberon also played nameless, uncredited roles in many films, such as in the movie The Three Passions (1928)," still begins with film work rather than her origin. Choice A, "Mara Oberon's story begins in Mumbai (then known as Bombay), India, in 1911," directly establishes where and when her life story begins. The answer is A.
